content: agentColor().dot returns bg-* Tailwind classes designed for CSS circles (empty spans with rounded-full). For text characters like ● use .text instead (text-* classes). Colors.dot is for backgrounds, colors.text is for text color.

New view integration in app.js requires 3 changes: (1) import at top after existing view imports, (2) case in Router switch, (3) entry in NAV_LINKS array. Strategy view follows issues.js filter button pattern with orange active/gray inactive style.

CoordinatorChat signal integration: read agentActivityLog.value in CoordinatorChat component body (not via useEffect/useState) — it's a Preact signal so accessing .value directly in render triggers reactive updates. Transform entries with spread + id/createdAt/_isAgentActivity before merging into allMessages.

tsx subprocess ESM fix: when generating a temp .ts script that uses top-level await and spawning via tsx, name the script with .mts extension instead of .ts — tsx treats .mts as ESM unconditionally, avoiding 'Top-level await not supported with cjs output format' error that occurs when the temp dir has no package.json with type:module

Playwright smoke test pattern for SPA browser JS: consolidate all route checks into ONE test (not one per route) to avoid repeated port-4174 server startup/teardown cycles. Use page.on/page.off per-route within a single test to isolate error capture per route. Filters: isActionableConsoleError() for console events, isActionablePageError() for pageerror events — both must be filtered independently since pre-existing ReferenceErrors arrive via pageerror not console.

CostsView self-managed time filtering: CostsView fetches its own time-filtered data (filteredMetrics, modelData, dateData) when a time window is selected, falling back to initial props for All Time. This keeps the feature self-contained and avoids adding global signals to state.js. Pattern: local useState + useEffect that fires on timeWindow change.

ModelBreakdown component pattern: uses modelColor() helper that matches model name substrings (opus/sonnet/haiku) to Tailwind color classes (blue-500/green-500/yellow-500/gray-500). Total tokens = inputTokens+outputTokens+cacheReadTokens+cacheCreationTokens. Shows horizontal bars scaled to max cost + session count legend below.

DateChart component pattern: CSS-based vertical bar chart using flex layout. Max bar height constant (160px), bars scale to maxCost. formatDateShort() formats date strings as MM/DD by splitting on '-' after extracting 'YYYY-MM-DD' portion. Shows cost label above bar, date label below.

SPA view components in views/ should self-fetch their data via useEffect rather than receiving data as props from the Router component in app.js. The Router reads appState signal values and passes them as props, but Preact signals auto-tracking may not reliably trigger Router re-renders when signals update asynchronously. Self-fetching views (AutopilotView, StrategyView, CommandView mail polling) are resilient; prop-receiving views (IssuesView) are fragile.

Router key pattern for SPA view switching: in app.js Layout, the Router component must have key=${view} to force Preact to fully unmount/remount on view changes. Without this key, Preact Signals subscription tracking in the Router (which reads signal values in dashboard/costs cases but not others) can interfere with proper component lifecycle on hash navigation, causing views like StrategyView to render blank.

RawChatView in src/server/public/views/raw-chat.js: 2-panel layout (session sidebar + chat area), self-fetches data via useEffect, uses fetchJson/postJson from lib/api.js, optimistic send UI, smart scroll with isNearBottomRef. Visually distinct from agent ChatView — no agent badges, task sidebar, or thread grouping. Nav link at #chat in app.js.

E2E test files must stay synchronized with app.js routing changes (NAV_LINKS, Router cases, default route). When renaming routes (#issues to #tasks), removing routes (#command merged), or adding routes (#chat), tests must update corresponding test assertions and ROUTES arrays. Backward-compat redirects can mask test failures by passing silently.

CostsView useEffect always-fetch pattern: remove early return for null timeWindow; always fetch /api/metrics, /api/metrics/by-model, /api/metrics/by-date with or without since param. safeMetrics uses filteredMetrics as primary with initialMetrics as pre-fetch fallback. select value must use String(timeWindow) not raw null to keep Preact select controlled. Model/date chart visibility is data-driven (modelData.length > 0, dateData.length > 0) not gated on timeWindow.

Browser JS Preact select value binding for nullable state: use String(stateVar) not raw stateVar as the value prop. When stateVar is null, String(null)='null' keeps the select controlled (matches option value='null'). Raw null removes the value prop, making the select uncontrolled, causing potential issues on controlled/uncontrolled transitions.
BeadIssue normalizeIssue prefers newer bd field names: use 'raw.owner ?? raw.assignee' for assignee, 'raw.blocked_by ?? raw.blockedBy' for blockedBy — supports both old and new bd CLI output formats.

issues.js categorize(): status==='blocked' must be checked explicitly BEFORE the 'open && hasBlockers' fallback — bd can return blocked as an explicit status value, not just derived from blockedBy array.

Browser JS Preact polling pattern: extract fetch into named function, call immediately on mount, setInterval(fn, 5000) for periodic refresh, cleanup returns cancelled=true AND clearInterval(interval). Used in dashboard.js and issues.js.

Browser JS useEffect with signal dep: use [appState.someSignal.value] as dep array to trigger signal-driven effects. Reading .value during render subscribes the component; the effect fires on re-render when signal changes. Clear signal inside effect with signalRef.value = null and guard with if (!ctx) return.

Root-user guard pattern: isRunningAsRoot() defined once in src/errors.ts (returns typeof process.getuid === 'function' && process.getuid() === 0) and imported in each spawn command. Guard throws ValidationError{field:'uid'} before config loading. No-op on non-POSIX (Windows). Tests mock process.getuid = () => 0 directly (no mock.module()).

name: Task detail view pattern
description: Clickable issue cards navigate to #task/:taskId. Detail view uses 5-tab interface (Overview, Agents, Communication, Events, Merge) with async polling. Data discovery: filter agents by beadId, mail by task mention, events by agent list, merge entries by branch name. Reference InspectView for async patterns, RawChatView for layout.

name: Task detail data discovery APIs
description: APIs for task detail view: /api/issues/:id (metadata), /api/agents (list), /api/agents/:name/inspect + /api/agents/:name/events (agent-specific), /api/mail + /api/mail/thread/:threadId (communication), /api/events (timeline), /api/merge-queue (branch status). Use beadId field from sessions to correlate agents with tasks.

content: Legio uses hash-based routing: #view or #view/param. Examples: #inspect/agentName, #task/issueId, #chat (no param). App.js Router component receives {view, param} from parseHash(). Add new cases to Router switch statement. IssueCard.onClick can navigate via location.hash = `#task/${issueId}`.

SPA view components should self-fetch their data via useEffect rather than receiving props from the Router component in app.js. Preact signals auto-tracking may not reliably trigger Router re-renders when signals update asynchronously. Self-fetching views are resilient; prop-receiving views are fragile.

CoordinatorChat persistent history pattern: coordinator-chat.js exports CoordinatorChat({ mail, headless }) — fetches GET /api/coordinator/chat/history?limit=100 on mount + polls every 5s, sends via POST /api/coordinator/chat (not /api/terminal/send), merges historyMessages+pendingMessages+activityEntries+coordMessages with Set-based id dedup, clears thinking+deduplicates pending when coordinator response detected in either history (historyFromCoordCount) or mail (fromCoordCount).

Worktree biome lint limitation: biome.json excludes !**/.legio so biome cannot lint files within worktree paths at .legio/worktrees/*. Quality gate for browser JS view files in worktrees must rely on manual review against patterns established in canonical src/ files (e.g. command.js). Typecheck still works since tsc processes .ts files only.

TerminalPanel sub-component pattern: collapsible panel with own state (expanded, streamText, baselineCaptureRef), polls via /api/terminal/capture when thinking OR expanded, auto-scrolls with useRef+useEffect on streamText, clears state when neither thinking nor expanded

CoordinatorChat component extraction pattern: dashboard.js imports CoordinatorChat from coordinator-chat.js; coordinator-chat.js owns stripAnsi, diffCapture, SLASH_COMMANDS, TerminalPanel, and CoordinatorChat; dashboard.js keeps only MetricsStrip, MergeQueue, AgentRoster, CoordinatorBar, DashboardView

TerminalPanel sub-component pattern: collapsible panel with own state (expanded, streamText, baselineCaptureRef), polls via setInterval when thinking=true OR expanded=true, clears on chatTarget change via useEffect dep array.

CoordinatorChat component extraction: dashboard.js imports CoordinatorChat from coordinator-chat.js; dashboard.js drops agentActivityLog, groupActivityMessages, isActivityMessage, renderMarkdown, useRef imports after extraction.

Chat redesign decomposition: single builder suffices for tightly coupled extraction (coordinator-chat.js + dashboard.js). No scout needed when lead reads all files directly and patterns are well-understood from mulch expertise.

Chat target switch guard pattern: use useRef(false) as manualSelectionRef to prevent auto-select useEffect from overriding explicit user clicks. Set ref=true in onClick handlers, reset to false when both agents run simultaneously. Apply to any auto-select that should yield to manual choice.

Chat target message filtering pattern: tag optimistic pending messages with _chatTarget at creation time; filter historyMessages and pendingMessages per chatTarget before the allMessages merge loop. Prevents cross-target message leakage when switching between coordinator and gateway views.

ChatView conversation mode pattern: selectedConversation state (null | {type, name, label, capability?, state?}) drives sidebar Conversations section, message feed merge (history+mail+pending deduped by id), and chat input routing to /api/{coordinator,gateway}/chat or /api/agents/:name/chat. When set, clears selectedTask/selectedAgent, fetches history via useEffect, locks To field, hides Chat/All toggle. Graceful 404 handling for undeployed backends.

Optimistic message pattern in ChatView conversation mode: create pending message with id=pending-${Date.now()}, push to pendingConvMessages, POST to API, on error filter it back out using the captured id, on success keep it until next poll deduplicates by id.

TerminalPanel uses baselineRef for diff-based terminal streaming: first poll captures full pane, subsequent polls use diffCapture() to compute delta (1500ms interval). Baseline resets when chatTarget changes to prevent spurious output.

Message deduplication in CoordinatorChat: track fromTargetCount and historyFromCoordCount separately. When either increases, clear thinking state and filter pending messages that match responses (identical body + createdAt within 60s window) to prevent duplicate display.

chat.js history polling pattern: extract fetchHistory as named function inside useEffect, call immediately then setInterval(fetchHistory, 3000). Return () => clearInterval(intervalId) as cleanup. Enables real-time response pickup without page reload.

coordinator-chat.js mapHistoryMessage uses from/to/body MailMessage fields (not role/content chat-history format) since unified history returns MailMessage objects directly from mail.db; maps from='human' to display as 'you'.

AgentRoster hierarchy pattern: buildAgentHierarchy(agents) returns depth-annotated [{agent, depth}] list. Roots = agents with no parentAgent or whose parentAgent is not in the agent list. Children placed after parent, sorted by state. Render with style={{ marginLeft: depth * 16 + 'px' }} for indentation.

CoordinatorChat simplified to unified timeline: fetches GET /api/chat/unified/history?limit=200, no Coordinator/Gateway toggle, no chatTarget state. Messages from='human' show right-aligned as 'You'; other from values show left-aligned with agent name label. Props: coordRunning only (mail/gwRunning dropped from usage). TerminalPanel kept with fixed chatTarget='coordinator'.

Two-panel planning layout: flex container with left panel flex-[3] min-w-0 (~60%) and right panel w-80 for ideas sidebar; GatewayChat component extracts GatewayView as reusable embedded panel with onStatusChange callback.

transcript-sync polling pattern in coordinator-chat.js: useEffect with thinking dep, poll POST /api/chat/transcript-sync every 2s when thinking=true, 10s when false, plus one-shot on mount; swallow errors silently (transcript may not exist yet)

thinking timeout pattern in coordinator-chat.js: useRef(null) for thinkingTimeoutRef, useEffect on [thinking] to set 120s setTimeout clearing setThinking(false); cleanup clears timeout on each re-run to prevent multiple concurrent timeouts

TerminalPanel loading state pattern: add const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false); set setLoading(true) before starting the poll interval; call setLoading(false) inside pollCapture after a successful fetch response (guarded by !cancelled); reset setLoading(false) in both the chatTarget change useEffect and the !thinking && !expanded early-return cleanup path. Render order: streamText → output, loading → animate-pulse placeholder, else → empty state.

MailFeed click-to-expand pattern: add expandedId useState (null|id), toggleExpand useCallback, wrap each item in outer div with key; inner div gets onClick+cursor-pointer; conditional expanded section below shows body (pre-wrap mono), priority (if \!== 'normal', yellow), payload (JSON.stringify). Filter audience=human from sorted array before display.
GatewayChat import in dashboard.js uses './gateway-chat.js' (same views/ directory), not '../components/gateway-chat.js'. The component accepts gwRunning={boolean} prop, not onStatusChange callback.

gateway-chat.js lives in src/server/public/views/ (same directory as dashboard.js and coordinator-chat.js). Import from dashboard.js is './gateway-chat.js'. GatewayChat accepts { gwRunning } boolean prop, not an onStatusChange callback.

When moving optimistic UI state updates (setInput, setThinking) before an await in gateway-chat.js handleSend, the catch block does not restore input text on error — the 120s auto-clear timeout for thinking state serves as the safety net. Verify error-path UX when relocating state updates before async calls.

gateway-chat.js pending message gap fix: do NOT remove optimistic pending on POST success. Instead, add a separate useEffect watching historyMessages that deduplicates pending entries (same from=human, same body, createdAt within 60s). The fromAgentCount useEffect is not sufficient because it only fires when agent replies arrive — the history useEffect fires on every poll, catching confirmed messages promptly.

gateway-chat.js adaptive history polling: history useEffect depends on 'thinking' state. When thinking=true poll every 2000ms, when false poll every 5000ms. Adding 'thinking' to the dep array causes the interval to reset on state change, providing a clean teardown/re-setup cycle.

gateway-chat.js rapid submit race fix: use thinkingCountRef = useRef(0) to track outstanding sends. In handleSend: thinkingCountRef.current += 1 before setThinking(true). In fromAgentCount useEffect: decrement with Math.max(0, count-1) and only call setThinking(false) when count reaches 0. Auto-clear timeout (60s) also resets count to 0.

gateway-chat.js thinkingCountRef pattern: useRef(0) counter for concurrent sends. Increment before setThinking(true) in handleSend, decrement with Math.max(0,...) in fromAgentCount useEffect, only clear thinking when count reaches 0. Auto-clear timeout (60s) resets count to 0. Failed sends do not decrement — timeout handles cleanup.

gateway-chat.js history-based pending dedup: independent useEffect watching historyMessages removes pending entries when a matching history entry appears (same body, from=human, createdAt within 60s). Separate from fromAgentCount useEffect which handles agent-reply triggered dedup. Both paths are idempotent.

gateway-chat.js adaptive poll interval: history useEffect depends on thinking state. Uses 2000ms interval when thinking=true, 5000ms when false. Adding thinking to dependency array ensures interval resets cleanly on state change via cleanup function.

gateway-chat.js WS push pattern: useEffect watching [appState.mail.value] filters for gateway-relevant messages (from/to gateway or human, audience human|both) and merges into historyMessages by dedup on id. Fallback poll changed from 5000ms to 30000ms.

POST routes in routes.ts must be placed BEFORE the GET-only guard (line ~1103: 'if (request.method \!== GET) return 405'). All POST endpoints after this guard cause TS error TS2367 because method is narrowed to GET. Pattern: add section header '// Section — POST route (before the GET-only guard)' and put the route before the guard.

IssuesView search filter pattern: add searchInput state + debounceRef=useRef(null), handleSearchChange useCallback that sets searchInput immediately and debounces setSearchText(val) via setTimeout(200ms). Filter issues via matchSearch(issue, query) checking id/title/description/status and 'p{priority}' string. Add search input UI above priority filter bar. Debounce avoids excessive re-renders on fast typing.

DispatchableCard wrapper pattern in issues.js: wrap IssueCard with a div, add dispatching/dispatched/dispatchError state, show Dispatch button only for open/in_progress issues, use e.stopPropagation() in handler to prevent card click navigation. Button shows loading/success states. Error shown inline next to button. Column component uses DispatchableCard instead of IssueCard directly.

IssuesView search filter pattern: searchInput (raw) + searchText (debounced via 200ms setTimeout in useRef). matchSearch() checks id/title/description/status/priority against lowercased query. Filter chain: searchText → priorityFilter → showClosed toggle.

gateway-chat.js coordinator toggle pattern: add showCoordinator useState(false) + coordinatorMessages useState([]). Separate useEffect polls /api/coordinator/chat/history when toggle is on (clears array when off). WS push useEffect updated with showCoordinator dep to also capture coordinator<->human messages. Merge section conditionally includes coordinatorMessages in spread. Toggle button uses purple accent classes when active (border-purple-600 text-purple-400 bg-purple-900/20) vs dim when inactive.

Coordinator message visual distinction in gateway-chat.js: detect isCoordinator = msg.from === 'coordinator' || msg.to === 'coordinator'. Agent coordinator messages use bg-purple-900/20 border border-purple-800/40. Human->coordinator use bg-purple-600/20 border border-purple-600/30. Coordinator label badge (bg-purple-900/40 text-purple-400) shown next to sender name for non-user coordinator messages. Sender name uses text-purple-400 for coordinator messages.

IssueCard children prop pattern: IssueCard accepts { issue, children } and renders ${children} at the bottom of the card div (after blockedBy row). Callers can pass additional UI (like dispatch button) as children to render inside the card boundary. Close with <//> in htm syntax. undefined children render as nothing.
Buttons inside IssueCard onClick containers must call e.stopPropagation() to prevent card navigation. The dispatch button in DispatchableCard correctly does this.
biome ignores .legio/worktrees/* so lint on worktree files must be done from canonical repo targeting specific paths or by comparing diff against baseline.
issues.js has pre-existing lint errors (unused 'h' import, useTemplate style) that predate PRs and should not block review.

AgentRoster Details button pattern: insert <a href='#inspect/${agent.agentName}'> with e.stopPropagation() between beadId span and expand arrow span. Use class 'text-xs text-blue-400 hover:text-blue-300 flex-shrink-0 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded bg-blue-900/20 hover:bg-blue-900/40 no-underline'. This avoids the two-click UX of expand-then-click-link.
AgentRoster Details button pattern: insert <a href=#inspect/agentName> with e.stopPropagation() before the expand arrow span, after the beadId span in the agent row

Tab title dynamic update pattern in app.js: in the fetchJson('/api/setup/status').then() callback, set document.title before setIsInitialized. Use 'Legio \u2014 ' + data.projectName (em dash U+2014) guarded by if (data.projectName) so default title from index.html is preserved when projectName is absent.

Tab title dynamic update pattern in app.js: in fetchJson('/api/setup/status').then() callback, set document.title = 'Legio — ' + data.projectName guarded by if(data.projectName), placed before setIsInitialized.

SkeletonCard/SkeletonColumn pattern in issues.js: place before Column component (~line 122). SkeletonCard uses inline style animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite. SkeletonColumn renders Array.from({length:count}) of SkeletonCards. IssuesView early-return when fetchedIssues===null && (!signalIssues || signalIssues.length===0) with inline <style>@keyframes shimmer{} inside the return.

Close button in DispatchableCard: add closeConfirm (bool) + closing (bool) + closeError (null|string) states. handleClose uses two-click pattern: first click sets closeConfirm=true (shows 'Confirm?'), second executes postJson('/api/issues/:id/close'). On success, update appState.issues.value via map to set status='closed'. Style: border-red-700 text-red-400 hover:bg-red-900/20. Place button in same flex container as Dispatch button. Always call e.stopPropagation().

Close button on issue cards follows DispatchableCard pattern in issues.js: useState for closeConfirm/closing/closeError, two-click confirm (first click sets confirm state, second executes), e.stopPropagation() required, POST /api/issues/:id/close endpoint in routes.ts before GET-only guard.

issue-card.js dispatch button positioning investigated and confirmed correct: app.js uses Preact IssuesView (not legacy shim at line 63), children rendered inside outer div at line 91, CSS border-t mt-2 pt-2 spacing correct. Indentation in template literals has no DOM effect.

Close button default state color: DispatchableCard Close button uses border-[#666] text-[#999] hover:bg-[#666]/10 (neutral gray) to visually distinguish from Dispatch button (border-[#E64415] text-[#E64415] orange-red). Only the default (non-confirm, non-closing) state uses gray; confirm/closing states remain unchanged.
DispatchableCard Close button default state uses neutral gray (border-[#666] text-[#999] hover:bg-[#666]/10) instead of red; closeConfirm only changes button text label not CSS class

addStatusPrefixes post-processing pattern in message-bubble.js: apply after renderMarkdown in dangerouslySetInnerHTML to wrap [DONE],[ERROR],[INFO],[WARN],[PENDING],[MERGED] with colored spans using Tailwind classes (text-green-400, text-red-400, text-blue-400, text-yellow-400, text-[#999], text-purple-400 respectively)
isDispatchable in issues.js DispatchableCard should only include 'open' status so in-progress tasks cannot be re-dispatched from the UI.
components/gateway-chat.js renders termOutput as raw terminal text (whitespace-pre-wrap, not dangerouslySetInnerHTML), so markdown and status prefix styling do not apply to it.

When removing a feature toggle from a Preact component, verify all 7 locations: state declarations, fetch useEffect, WS useEffect (filter logic + merge block + dep array), mergeSource computation, JSX button, render-time variable, and conditional CSS classes.

When removing a coordinator toggle from gateway-chat.js, 7 locations must be cleaned: (1) coordinatorMessages + showCoordinator useState, (2) coordinator fetch useEffect, (3) coordMessages logic in WebSocket useEffect + showCoordinator dep removed, (4) mergeSource ternary simplified to [...historyMessages, ...pendingMessages], (5) toggle button in header, (6) isCoordinator variable in message renderer, (7) coordinator-specific bubbleClass branches and badge span.
